tasks.title: Group C - Benchmark Runner & Accuracy Gate
tasks.description: Build the benchmark runner, reporting surface, and CI accuracy gate that validate ScanMeNow detection against independent PHI/PII testing data. Task C consumes Task D extraction/OCR support, Task G PII capability, and Task H independent testing data.
tasks.notes: PII THRESHOLD POLICY (2026-05-27): Task C must define separate benchmark thresholds for each PII entity label instead of reusing PHI/HIPAA thresholds wholesale. Task G defines the activated PII label set, including CRYPTO and NRP; Task H supplies corpus coverage. Task C owns per-label PII recall/precision thresholds and should document any labels with no hard threshold or best-effort treatment. Thresholds are now written â€” read docs/benchmark_thresholds.json, which is the authoritative source the runner enforces. --- ARCHITECTURE NOTES (orchestrator, 2026-05-27): (1) EXTRACTION STACK: Task C's runner.py must import Task D's extraction stack directly (src/scanmenow/walker/reader.py) â€” do not duplicate extraction logic. Same code path = same extracted text = same offsets as corpus. (2) PDF LIBRARY: pdfplumber is authoritative for text-layer PDF extraction across the whole project. Task D uses it, Task H corpus was produced with it, Task C must inherit it via the shared reader. (3) CORPUS PATH: SCANMENOW_CORPUS_PATH env var sets corpus location; --corpus CLI arg overrides; pytest gate skips gracefully if env var unset (do not fail CI on missing corpus). (4) TOLERANCE: exact char offset match for text-native formats; Â±5 char tolerance for OCR-derived formats (PNG/JPG/TIFF/BMP/scanned PDF). --- ARCHITECTURAL DEPENDENCY CONTRACTS (2026-05-27): Task C is a CONSUMER of three cross-task contracts. CONTRACT 1 â€” build_analyzer() [Owner: Task #3 + Task G (#9)]: import build_analyzer() from src/scanmenow/detection/analyzer.py and call it once per benchmark run. Do NOT re-register recognizers inside runner.py. Task G (#9) will have already merged all 8 PII recognizers into this factory before Task C runs; they appear automatically. CONTRACT 2 â€” read_file_text(path) [Owner: Task D (#5)]: import read_file_text(path: Path) -> str from src/scanmenow/walker/reader.py and use it for all corpus text extraction in the benchmark runner. Do NOT duplicate any format-handling logic â€” this is the hard coupling that keeps corpus golden-finding offsets consistent with production scanning offsets. CONTRACT 3 â€” docs/benchmark_thresholds.json [Owner: Task C (#4), this task]: the runner reads this file at startup; no thresholds are hardcoded in runner.py. Adding a new entity type means adding a row to the JSON; the runner picks it up with no code change. min_recall: null + hard_threshold: false = best-effort, reported only, never fails CI.

objective.statement: Deliver a benchmark runner that loads an independent corpus, extracts text through Task D's shared reader, analyzes text through build_analyzer(), compares analyzer spans against golden findings and decoys, applies per-entity thresholds from docs/benchmark_thresholds.json, and exposes both a CLI command and pytest gate.
successCriteria: Threshold loader reads docs/benchmark_thresholds.json and supports hard-threshold and report-only labels
Span matcher supports exact text-native matching, +/-5 OCR tolerance, and decoy-aware false-positive accounting
Runner imports shared read_file_text(path) and build_analyzer() contracts instead of duplicating extraction or recognizer registration
scanmenow benchmark --corpus <path> [--output report.json] prints a terminal report and optionally writes JSON
tests/test_benchmark.py skips gracefully when corpus is absent and enforces configured hard thresholds when corpus is present
nonGoals: Producing the independent corpus, golden findings, or extracted text companions - Task H
Implementing file extraction, OCR, or format readers - Task D
Registering PHI/PII recognizers - Task G and prior HIPAA recognizer work
Installing Tesseract or LibreOffice - Task #12
Redaction, reduction, migration, retention, or UI work

tasks.title: Group D - Filesystem Walker & Integration
tasks.description: Local filesystem walker with path resolution, skip patterns, symlink policy, max-depth limits, and end-to-end CLI integration. File format scope: TXT, CSV, JSON, PDF (pdfplumber), DOCX/XLSX/PPTX (python-docx/openpyxl/python-pptx), legacy DOC (LibreOffice headless subprocess), PNG/JPG/JPEG/TIFF/BMP/GIF (pytesseract + Tesseract OCR). System dependencies: Tesseract OCR, LibreOffice. Platform scope: Windows and Linux.
tasks.notes: Hardening concern carried from Task #3 dual review (Claude Sonnet 4.6 + Codex GPT-5, 2026-05-26): when the filesystem walker pipes findings through the detection layer, expand the recognizer collision tests from 'at least one entity fires' into expected entity/span precedence behaviour. A numeric token can match MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER, ACCOUNT_NUMBER, and DEVICE_IDENTIFIER simultaneously; if downstream redaction or reporting logic needs deterministic entity precedence over overlapping PHI spans, that dedup/priority contract must be defined and tested here rather than left implicit. OCR SCOPE ADDITION (2026-05-27): Task D now includes OCR capability for image-format files (PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF) via Tesseract (pytesseract), and legacy .doc files via LibreOffice headless subprocess (cross-platform, no extra pip package). LibreOffice is a system dependency like Tesseract; absence triggers WARNING + skip rather than crash. Linux testing will be performed on WSL. INSTALLER NOTE: Task #12 (Dependency Bootstrapper) owns auto-detection and installation of Tesseract and LibreOffice for end users. --- ORCHESTRATOR CORRECTIONS (2026-05-27, cross-session sync from Task C/H planning): (1) PDF LIBRARY CORRECTION: use pdfplumber (not pdfminer.six) for text-layer PDF extraction. Task H corpus will generate .extracted.txt companions for text PDFs using pdfplumber per docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md â€” if Task D uses pdfminer.six instead, extracted text will differ and all text-layer PDF corpus offsets will drift in the benchmark. Update reader.py to use pdfplumber, page-by-page extraction joined with newline. (2) IMAGE FORMAT GAP: add .tiff/.tif and .bmp to SUPPORTED_EXTENSIONS and reader format handlers. The Task H corpus spec (docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md, correction #4 in Task H backlog notes commit 5e680077) requires doc_013_lab_result_scan.tiff and doc_014_consent_form_scan.bmp â€” these files will be in corpus/ and must be scannable. Both use the same pytesseract extraction path as PNG/JPG. Without them, two corpus documents produce zero findings and Task C benchmark shows 100% false-negative rate for all entity types in those files. --- ARCHITECTURAL DEPENDENCY CONTRACT (2026-05-27): Task D (#5) OWNS Contract 2. Task D must expose read_file_text(path: Path) -> str as a stable public function at src/scanmenow/walker/reader.py. Task C (benchmark runner) imports this function directly â€” it does not duplicate format-handling logic. Interface contract: returns extracted plain text (str); returns empty string + logs WARNING on graceful skip (missing Tesseract, missing LibreOffice); raises on unrecoverable errors. This is the hard coupling that keeps corpus golden-finding offsets consistent with production scanning offsets â€” any drift between Task D's extraction and Task C's extraction invalidates benchmark accuracy numbers in both directions. The extraction libraries and commands specified in docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md are authoritative for this function.

objective.statement: Deliver a working filesystem walker that scans all supported file formats (TXT, CSV, JSON, PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, DOC, PNG, JPG, JPEG, TIFF, BMP, GIF) for PHI/PII, stores structured findings in SQLite with source_line provenance, and exposes `scanmenow scan <path>` as a working CLI command on Windows and Linux.

Windows/Linux/VMware installers (Tasks #13-15)
Legacy .xls, .xlsb, .ppt binary formats
Streaming or chunked scanning for files >100MB
Cloud storage scanning (Task #8)
Reduction or redaction pipeline
Electron UI integration

tasks.title: Group F - Phase 1 Exit Gate & Verification
tasks.description: Accuracy threshold validation, CLI functional tests on real fixture directory, documentation, and Phase 1 completion (1 subtask)

tasks.title: Group E - Cloud & Retention Services
tasks.description: Firestore connection layer with environment flag for local dev, retention policy evaluation, and cloud integration (4 subtasks)
tasks.notes: STORAGE LAYER DEPENDENCY (orchestrator, 2026-05-27): Task #8 refactors storage into a Repository(Protocol) + SqliteRepository class (storage/base.py). Task #5 extends the same storage module: adds findings.source_line via ALTER TABLE and adds get_overlapping_findings() to repository. If Task #8 builds before Task #5, SqliteRepository will be created without wrapping get_overlapping_findings(), leaving the Repository protocol incomplete. Task #5 must land first (priority 97 > 96 already enforces order), OR Task #8 must include get_overlapping_findings() in the Repository protocol and SqliteRepository on the assumption that Task #5 adds it. Dependency [5] added to enforce correct build order. --- Also note: Task #8 adds findings.created_at column via ALTER TABLE; Task #5 adds findings.source_line via ALTER TABLE. Both are idempotent. The order does not matter for the migrations themselves, only for the Repository class wrapper.

objective.statement: Deliver a Firestore-backed cloud storage layer with a SCANMENOW_BACKEND env flag (sqlite|firestore), an age-based retention policy evaluator, and a `scanmenow retain` CLI subcommand â€” all independently testable without a live GCP connection.

FirestoreRepository lazily imports google-cloud-firestore so SQLite mode never requires GCP credentials
evaluate_retention() dry-run returns RetentionReport with expired_count and zero deleted_count without deleting any findings
evaluate_retention() live mode deletes only findings older than max_age_days; findings with empty created_at are conservatively kept
scanmenow retain --max-age-days 90 --dry-run exits 0 and prints a retention report

Legal holds and approval gates â€” follow-up backlog task
Archive to cold storage instead of delete â€” follow-up backlog task
Firestore emulator integration tests
Firestore security rules configuration
Real-time Firestore listeners or streaming
Multi-region Firestore setup

tasks.title: Task G - Add PII to Scope and Capability
tasks.description: Add PII scanning to the project scope and detection capability alongside PHI scanning. Task G defines the expanded PII entity set that Task C must benchmark and Task H must represent in independent testing data.
tasks.notes: TASK G BUILD NOTES (orchestrator, 2026-05-27): LOCKED PII LABEL SET â€” 8 US-focused Presidio built-in types: CREDIT_CARD, US_BANK_NUMBER, US_PASSPORT, US_DRIVER_LICENSE, IBAN_CODE, CRYPTO, MEDICAL_LICENSE, NRP. International PII (AU, UK, SG, EU) deferred to Task #16. No custom recognizers needed â€” all 8 are Presidio built-ins. --- PHASE 1 GATE (critical): before writing any code, audit the installed Presidio package to verify exact recognizer class names and entity type strings. Wrong class names cause silent misses â€” the integration test (engine.get_recognizers()) is the verification gate. --- REQUIRED DELIVERABLES: (1) src/scanmenow/detection/recognizers/pii/__init__.py + us_pii.py â€” exports ALL_PII_RECOGNIZERS; (2) src/scanmenow/detection/recognizers/__init__.py updated â€” merges ALL_PII_RECOGNIZERS into ALL_RECOGNIZERS; (3) docs/pii_coverage.json â€” 8-entry registry mirroring hipaa_coverage.json format: entity_type, Presidio recognizer class, confidence notes, jurisdiction, collision_risk; (4) docs/registries/entity-types.md â€” 8 new entries following existing format (Task H coverage matrix depends on this being up to date before corpus production starts); (5) tests/test_pii_recognizers.py â€” positive + negative per type (16 tests min) + integration test confirming all 8 registered. --- COLLISION DOCUMENTATION (required in pii_coverage.json): US_DRIVER_LICENSE may overlap with CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER; MEDICAL_LICENSE may overlap with MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Both are expected and Presidio handles them â€” document collision_risk field per entity. --- NRP NOTE: Presidio NRP recognizer detects nationality/religion/political group mentions in text (e.g. 'identifies as Catholic', 'US citizen', 'Republican Party member'). It is keyword/phrase detection, not a structured ID format. Document as best-effort in pii_coverage.json. Task C assigns NRP no hard accuracy threshold (same as BIOMETRIC_IDENTIFIER). --- DOWNSTREAM DEPENDENCIES: Task H corpus production is waiting on entity-types.md to be updated before it can finalize its coverage matrix. Task C benchmark thresholds for all 8 PII types are already written in docs/benchmark_thresholds.json â€” read it for context. --- ARCHITECTURAL DEPENDENCY CONTRACT (2026-05-27): Task G (#9) OWNS Contract 1. All 8 PII recognizers must be registered into build_analyzer() in src/scanmenow/detection/analyzer.py â€” NOT into a separate factory or alternate entry point. build_analyzer() is the single factory for the AnalyzerEngine. Task C imports build_analyzer() as-is and calls it once per benchmark run; it does not know about PII recognizers specifically. Any recognizer Task G merges into ALL_RECOGNIZERS is automatically benchmarked by Task C on the next run with no code change in Task C. This means Task G must merge into src/scanmenow/detection/recognizers/__init__.py (ALL_RECOGNIZERS list) before Task C's first full benchmark run. Do not expose a separate build_pii_analyzer() or similar â€” Task C will not call it.

objective.statement: Deliver 8 US-focused PII entity types (CREDIT_CARD, US_BANK_NUMBER, US_PASSPORT, US_DRIVER_LICENSE, IBAN_CODE, CRYPTO, MEDICAL_LICENSE, NRP) registered in build_analyzer() alongside existing HIPAA recognizers, with a pii_coverage.json registry, entity-types.md extension, and a passing test suite.
successCriteria: docs/pii_coverage.json documents all 8 US PII entity types with entity_type, presidio_class, jurisdiction, confidence_notes, and collision_risk
ALL_PII_RECOGNIZERS exported from src/scanmenow/detection/recognizers/pii/us_pii.py and merged into ALL_RECOGNIZERS
build_analyzer() returns an AnalyzerEngine with all 8 PII entity type strings registered alongside the 7 HIPAA custom recognizers
tests/test_pii_recognizers.py passes: 8 positive, 8 negative, 1 integration test (17 tests minimum)
docs/registries/entity-types.md extended with 8 new PII entity type entries
nonGoals: International PII entity types â€” Task #16
Filesystem walker integration â€” Task #5
Custom regex PII recognizers for types not in Presidio built-ins
End-to-end file scanning validation â€” Task #4

build_analyzer() registers all 8 PII entity type strings
tests/test_pii_recognizers.py passes: 8 positive, 8 negative, 1 integration
docs/registries/entity-types.md extended with 8 new entries

stopConditions: If any of the 8 target Presidio recognizer classes are absent from the installed Presidio version, stop and resolve the version mismatch before proceeding â€” do not write us_pii.py with unverified class names

tasks.plan:
## Overview

Activate 8 US-focused PII entity types from Presidio's built-in recognizer library, merging them into the existing build_analyzer() factory that already carries 7 custom HIPAA Safe Harbor recognizers. No custom regex needed â€” all 8 are Presidio built-ins.

## Phases

Phase 1 â€” PII Coverage Registry: Audit installed Presidio to confirm class names; produce docs/pii_coverage.json.
Phase 2 â€” PII Recognizer Module: Create pii/ sub-package with us_pii.py exporting ALL_PII_RECOGNIZERS; merge into ALL_RECOGNIZERS in recognizers/__init__.py.
Phase 3 â€” Tests & Entity Registry: Write tests/test_pii_recognizers.py (positive + negative + integration); extend docs/registries/entity-types.md.

## First Proof Unit

Proof Unit 1: docs/pii_coverage.json exists with 8 entries.
Command: uv run python -c "import json, pathlib; d=json.loads(pathlib.Path('docs/pii_coverage.json').read_text()); assert len(d)==8; print(f'OK â€” {len(d)} PII types documented')"
Expected initial failure: FileNotFoundError.

proofUnits.title: Coverage registry documents all 8 US PII types
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: docs/pii_coverage.json exists with exactly 8 entries documenting entity type, Presidio class, jurisdiction, confidence notes, and collision risk.
proofUnits.exactCommand: uv run python -c "import json, pathlib; d=json.loads(pathlib.Path('docs/pii_coverage.json').read_text()); assert len(d)==8, f'Expected 8, got {len(d)}'; print(f'OK â€” {len(d)} PII types documented')"
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: FileNotFoundError â€” docs/pii_coverage.json does not exist yet.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: Prints 'OK â€” 8 PII types documented' and exits 0.

proofUnits.title: All 8 PII recognizers registered in build_analyzer()
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: build_analyzer() returns an AnalyzerEngine whose recognizer registry includes all 8 US PII entity type strings.
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'scanmenow.detection.recognizers.pii' or test file does not exist.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: 1 passed â€” all 8 strings (CREDIT_CARD, US_BANK_NUMBER, US_PASSPORT, US_DRIVER_LICENSE, IBAN_CODE, CRYPTO, MEDICAL_LICENSE, NRP) appear in the recognizer registry.

proofUnits.title: Positive detection tests pass for all 8 PII types
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: Each of the 8 PII entity types detects its target sample text with at least one RecognizerResult returned.
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: ModuleNotFoundError or test file does not exist â€” 0 tests collected.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: 8 passed â€” one positive assertion per entity type.

proofUnits.title: Full PII test suite passes
proofUnits.expectedBehavior: All PII recognizer tests pass â€” positive detections succeed, negative fixtures produce no false positives, integration test confirms all 8 types are active.
proofUnits.expectedInitialFailure: Multiple failures â€” recognizer modules and tests do not exist.
proofUnits.expectedPassingEvidence: 17+ tests passed â€” 8 positive, 8 negative, 1 integration. No failures or errors.

tasks.title: Task H - Independent Testing Data for Task C
tasks.description: Produce the independent synthetic PHI/PII testing corpus, companion extracted text files, golden findings JSON, near-miss decoys, and delivery notes that Task C will consume for validation, accuracy benchmarking, and regression guarding. This task must preserve independence by avoiding ScanMeNow source code, tests, and existing fixtures while authoring the corpus.
tasks.notes: PII ENTITY LABEL SET (from Task G, 2026-05-27): Task H corpus must include representative examples for all 8 US PII entity types activated by Task G: CREDIT_CARD (Luhn-validated card numbers), US_BANK_NUMBER (US routing/account numbers), US_PASSPORT (US passport numbers), US_DRIVER_LICENSE (US state driver's license numbers), IBAN_CODE (SWIFT/IBAN format bank account numbers), CRYPTO (cryptocurrency wallet addresses â€” Bitcoin, Ethereum, etc.), MEDICAL_LICENSE (medical license and NPI numbers), NRP (nationality/religion/political group mentions). These supplement the 15 PHI HIPAA Safe Harbor entity types already in scope. TOTAL LABEL SET: 23 distinct entity labels. --- SPEC CORRECTIONS AND CLARIFICATIONS (2026-05-27, from Task C planning session â€” read docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md for full detail): (1) COVERAGE REQUIREMENT CORRECTION: the '2 positive examples per PII type' figure in the original note is superseded â€” correct requirement is >=3 findings across >=2 documents for all 21 hard-threshold labels. BIOMETRIC_IDENTIFIER and NRP require only >=2 mentions with no hard accuracy threshold (best-effort, reported only). (2) THRESHOLD FILE: per-label recall thresholds are now in docs/benchmark_thresholds.json â€” this is the authoritative source the benchmark runner enforces. Read it before writing the corpus. (3) MEDICAL_LICENSE vs CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER OVERLAP: NPI numbers and state medical license numbers should appear as MEDICAL_LICENSE in golden_findings.json only â€” do not add CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER for the same span even if that recognizer would also fire. (4) MINIMUM DOCUMENTS: 15 documents minimum across all 13 required formats: txt, csv, docx, doc, xlsx, xls, pptx, pdf (text-layer), pdf (image-only/scanned), png, jpg, tiff, bmp. (5) OCR EXTRACTION: all image files and image-only PDFs must be extracted with pytesseract, lang='eng', default PSM, exactly as shown in docs/GROUP-C-CORPUS-SPEC.md â€” offsets in golden_findings.json reference the OCR-extracted text, not the raw image bytes. (6) COMPANION FILES: every non-plaintext, non-CSV document needs a .extracted.txt companion file produced by the library specified in the spec for that format (python-docx for .docx, LibreOffice headless for .doc, openpyxl for .xlsx, xlrd for .xls, python-pptx for .pptx, pdfplumber for text-layer PDF, pytesseract for images and scanned PDF). (7) NEAR-MISS DECOYS: at least 2 near-miss decoy entries per document recorded in the decoys[] array of golden_findings.json â€” not just avoided in the document text.

tasks.title: Build Bi-Temporal Progress Tracking
tasks.description: Add append-only task history so backlog changes can be queried by both project-effective time and system-recorded time while preserving docs/backlog.json as the current-state projection.

tasks.title: Dependency Bootstrapper - First-Run System Dependency Installer
tasks.description: On first Electron app launch, detect whether required system dependencies are installed (Tesseract OCR, LibreOffice headless) and silently install any that are missing. Separate install flows for Windows and Linux. Covers: version detection, silent download/install, progress feedback to the user, graceful failure with actionable error message if install is blocked (e.g. no internet, insufficient permissions). Must handle partial installs and re-check on subsequent launches. Option to bundle installers within the Electron package vs. stream from CDN on first run â€” decision affects package size vs. install reliability.
tasks.notes: System dependencies introduced by Task #5: Tesseract OCR (image scanning), LibreOffice headless (legacy .doc extraction). Both are system-level binaries, not pip packages. Windows install paths: Tesseract at C:\Program Files\Tesseract-OCR\tesseract.exe (winget: UB-Mannheim.TesseractOCR), LibreOffice at C:\Program Files\LibreOffice\program\soffice.exe. Linux install: apt-get install tesseract-ocr libreoffice (or equivalent). WSL-aware: distinguish WSL from native Linux for path resolution.

tasks.title: Windows Installer Package
tasks.description: Build a standalone Windows installer (.exe or .msi) for the ScanMeNow Electron app using Electron Builder. Package includes: embedded Python runtime (PyInstaller or uv-bundled), all pip dependencies, and the dependency bootstrapper (Task #12) that detects and installs Tesseract and LibreOffice on first run. Target: Windows 10/11 64-bit. Deliverable: signed installer that passes Windows SmartScreen, installs to Program Files, adds Start Menu entry and optional desktop shortcut.
tasks.notes: Electron Builder target: nsis (NSIS installer) or msi. Python embedding strategy TBD: PyInstaller bundled alongside Electron, or subprocess bridge to system Python. Code signing required for SmartScreen clearance. Consider auto-update via electron-updater.

tasks.title: Linux Installer Package
tasks.description: Build standalone Linux installer packages for the ScanMeNow Electron app. Targets: DEB (Ubuntu/Debian), RPM (RHEL/CentOS/Fedora), and AppImage (distribution-agnostic). Package includes: embedded Python runtime, all pip dependencies, and the dependency bootstrapper (Task #12) that detects and installs Tesseract and LibreOffice via the system package manager (apt, dnf, yum). Primary test environment: WSL (Ubuntu). Deliverable: three package artifacts and an install script.
tasks.notes: Electron Builder targets: deb, rpm, AppImage. WSL testing confirmed by Scott (2026-05-27). System package manager detection: prefer apt, fallback to dnf/yum/zypper. Bootstrapper must handle headless Linux installs (no GUI sudo prompt). Desktop entry (.desktop file) for GUI environments.

tasks.title: VMware VM Deployment Package
tasks.description: Build a pre-configured Linux VM image in OVF/OVA format with ScanMeNow fully installed and all system dependencies (Tesseract, LibreOffice) pre-configured. Targeted at enterprise deployments where application isolation, air-gapped environments, or IT-managed VM provisioning is required. Base OS: Ubuntu LTS (latest stable). Deliverable: OVA file importable into VMware vSphere, VMware Workstation, and VMware Fusion. Includes first-boot configuration script for network, hostname, and initial admin credentials.
tasks.notes: Base image: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (minimal server). VM spec: 4 vCPU, 8GB RAM, 60GB disk (minimum). VMware Tools pre-installed. ScanMeNow installed via Linux DEB package (Task #14 artifact). Consider also producing a VirtualBox OVA variant. Air-gap support: all dependency installers pre-bundled, no internet required post-deploy.

tasks.title: International PII Scope Expansion
tasks.description: Extend PII detection coverage beyond US-focused entity types to include international and GDPR-scoped identifiers. Activate Presidio's country-specific built-in recognizers: UK NHS number, AU TFN/Medicare/ABN/ACN, SG NRIC/FIN, EU fiscal codes (IT, ES, FI, PL), and any others supported by Presidio at implementation time. Produce a companion pii_international_coverage.json registry documenting each identifier, jurisdiction, recognizer source, and confidence notes. Depends on Task #9 (US PII foundation) being production.
tasks.notes: Deferred from Task #9 scope decision (2026-05-27): Scott confirmed US-only PII scope for Task #9. International expansion added as a separate backlog item for future consideration. No custom recognizers expected â€” all international types are Presidio built-ins requiring only registration.
